author: doll-agent
content: Add integer SIMD kernels at simd_i32.rs / simd_i64.rs covering add/sub/mul + bitwise and/or/xor (6 ops × 2 dtypes = 12 functions). Pulp's cross-platform Simd trait doesn't expose generic integer arithmetic — only per-backend platform methods (Sse2/Avx2/Aarch64-specific). Take the same auto-vectorization-via-tight-scalar-loop approach the existing f32/f64 binary kernels use (numpy's loops_arithmetic.dispatch.c.src does the same — compiler-driven, not intrinsic-driven). Wrapping semantics match Rust signed-overflow + numpy two's-complement convention. 12 tests covering basic ops, partial tails, overflow wrapping, and bitwise.
